label: Table 3
title: Comparison of intensity of behavioural addictions between OCD patients and healthy controls.
source: Rai et al., 2022, Psychiatry Research Communications
footnote: a Excluded from analysis were the BAs with very low baseline prevalence. P_a_: Mann–Whitney U (two-tailed), p < 0.05. Scores are instrument totals/derived scores as per each measure.

columns.role: Continuous severity from the named scale—not just pass/fail, but how high scores went
Average ± spread, plus median and middle 50% range—helpful when a few extreme scores pull the mean
Same summary pattern for healthy controls
Mann–Whitney U p-value: small p means one group’s scores tend to rank higher overall

cellGuide: Table 2 asked yes/no by cut-off; this table asks “even if rates looked similar, was symptom intensity higher?” Because scores were skewed, the authors used Mann–Whitney U (rank-based) instead of a t-test. Median and quartiles tell you where the typical person sat; p tells you if the two groups’ overall score piles differ.
